title: A highly-sensitive sandwich ELISA procedure based on one-step antibody immobilization for potential in vitro diagnostics
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"x2z2x","text":"A highly-sensitive sandwich enzyme-linked immunosorbent (ELISA) procedure was developed for detecting human fetuin A (HFA), a specific biomarker for a plethora of various infection and inflammation associated diseases. In this one-step antibody (Ab) immobilization, the capture anti-HFA Ab formed a stable complex with 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane (APTES), via ionic and hydrophobic interactions. The complex adsorbed on a microtiter plate (MTP) as an ELISA platform for HFA, and exhibited linearity from 4.9 pg mL-1 to 20 ng mL-1, a limit of detection of 7 pg mL-1 and an analytical sensitivity of 10 pg mL-1, i.e. 51-fold more sensitive than the commercial sandwich ELISA kit. The precision obtained by this procedure for the detection of HFA spiked in human whole blood and plasma was in agreement with the commercial kit. The anti-HFA Ab-bound MTPs stored at 4°C in 0.1 M phosphate-buffered saline, pH 7.4 displayed no significant decrease in original activities after two months.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":514,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":528,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":563,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":607,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"xijmu","text":"With high-throughput and precision, excellent reproducibility, and high sensitivity, ELISA serves a gold standard for in vitro diagnostics (IVD) in healthcare, industry and other diversified analytical settings. In the last 50 years, over 300,000 peer-reviewed articles related to ELISA have been published, attesting its unmatched analytical performance. There are numerous advanced and improved ELISA formats for IVD, which employ improved immobilization chemistry, signal enhancement strategy, lab-on-a-chip technology, biosensing and new immunoassay formats. Regardless of the platform, the performance of such analytical platforms is dependent upon the selection of an appropriate Ab immobilization strategy1-3. To date, various immobilization schemes have been considered and tested:  physical adsorption, oriented binding, covalent binding, click chemistry, site-directed immobilization and non-covalent binding4-10. In general, such immobilization protocols involve a complex, multi-step procedure with costly reagents and additional instruments. \r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":118,"length":8},{"style":"sup","offset":712,"length":3},{"style":"sup","offset":918,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"k5are","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"kfeuk","text":"This protocol is a highly-simplified one-step Ab immobilization-based sandwich ELISA procedure11 towards the development of rapid, cost-effective and highly-sensitive IVD kits. The dilution of capture Ab in APTES forms a stable APTES-polymer/Ab complex that is sorbed onto the MTP for the detection of HFA. HFA serves as a model analyte because all the sandwich immunoassay components are commercially-available in the form of a kit. The reduced amount of HFA is a specific biomarker for acute infectious and inflammatory diseases12. In contrast, an elevated level of HFA correlates with adiposity13, metabolic disease14, cardiovascular dysfunction15, atherosclerosis (related to vascular calcification with increased vascular stiffness)16, diabetes17, metabolic syndrome18,19, arthritis20 and malaria21. The determination of HFA is also highly relevant in hepatocellular carcinoma22, breast cancer23 and neurological diseases including multiple sclerosis24. The results obtained by the new immobilization format (NIF) were superior to the commercial conventional immobilization format (CIF) and our previously-developed covalent immobilization format (CovIF)-based ELISA procedures25. Its applicability for the detection of HFA in PBS and HFA spiked in diluted human whole blood and plasma compared favorably with the conventional and covalent crosslinking-based ELISA procedure25. Excellent storage stability of anti-HFA Ab-bound MTPs at 4°C in 0.1 M PBS, pH 7.4 is also demonstrated, illustrating the potential application of this novel immobilization strategy.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":94,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":530,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":597,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":618,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":648,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":737,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":749,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":771,"length":5},{"style":"sup","offset":787,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":801,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":881,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":898,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":955,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":1182,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":1379,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Mix anti-HFA capture Ab (8 µg mL -1 ) with 1% (v/v) APTES in the ratio of 1:1 (v/v). Incubate each of the desired wells of a 96-well MTP with 100 μL of the freshly prepared anti-HFA capture Ab solution, with a final concentration of 4 µg mL -1 in 0.5% APTES, for 30 min at RT. Wash five times with 300 μL of 0.1M PBS, pH 7.4. Washing can also be performed with an automatic plate washer.

Block the Ab-bound wells with 300 µL of 1% (w/v) BSA for 30 min at 37°C followed by extensive PBS washing (as mentioned in step 1). CRITICAL STEP Use filtered BSA to remove any microbial or other contaminants. ?TROUBLESHOOTING
Add 100 µL of varying HFA concentrations (4.9 pg mL -1 -20 ng mL -1 ) to different BSA-blocked wells. Incubate for 1 h at 37°C and wash extensively with PBS (as stated in step 1).
Add 100 µL of biotinylated anti-HFA Ab (200 ng mL -1 ) in each of the HFA-captured wells. Incubate for 1 h at 37°C and wash extensively with PBS (as mentioned in step 1).

Add 100 µL of streptavidin-HRP conjugate (diluted 1:200) to the biotinylated anti-HFA Ab treated wells. Incubate for 20 min at 37°C and wash extensively with PBS (as mentioned in step 1). ?TROUBLESHOOTING
Add 100 µL of TMB-H 2 O 2 mixture to each of these wells and incubate at RT to develop color for 20 min. ?TROUBLESHOOTING
Stop the enzyme-substrate reaction by adding 50 µL of 1 N H 2 SO 4 to each well.

Determine the absorbance at a primary wavelength of 450 nm taking 540 nm as the reference wavelength in the Tecan Infinite M200 Pro microplate reader. CRITICAL STEP Determine the absorbance within 10 min of stopping the enzyme substrate reaction.
Steps 1-2, Ab immobilization and BSA blocking: 1 h Steps 3-8, HFA sandwich ELISA: 2 h 45 min
Troubleshooting advice is provided in Table 1 .

The NIF shown in Figure 1 detected HFA in the range of 4.9 pg mL -1 - 20 ng mL -1 with linearity from 1.6 - 20 ng mL -1 (Figure 2a). The estimated limit of detection (LOD) and analytical sensitivity 6,25-27 were 7 and 10 pg mL -1 , respectively. Various experimental process controls were investigated to determine the efficiency of BSA blocking; non-specific interaction of BSA with HFA, biotinylated anti-HFA Ab and SA-HRP; and, non-specific interaction of capture anti-HFA Ab with biotinylated anti-HFA Ab. In general, the detection was highly specific for HFA without any interference of immunological reagents (Figure 2b). The intra- and inter-day variability ranged from 1.2 - 8.5 % and 2.1 - 10.2 %, respectively, with a half-effective concentration (EC50) of 2.6 ng mL -1 . The intra- and inter-day variability was calculated from five assay repeats (in triplicate) on a single and five different days, respectively. A comparison of the NIF versus the commercial CIF and CovIF-based sandwich ELISAs is presented in Table 2. The NIF significantly decreased the overall assay time from 20 h to ~4 h. Its analytical sensitivity is 10 pg mL -1 , i.e., 51-fold lower than the commercial CIF (510 pg mL -1 ). It also enabled 28-fold faster Ab immobilization than CIF. Moreover, it has a better LOD and analytical sensitivity in comparison to the HFA sandwich ELISA 6,25 based on heterobifunctional crosslinking of Ab to APTES-functionalized platforms, and surface plasmon resonance (SPR)-based immunoassay formats 1,28 . All the ELISA formats were performed under the same conditions with the same assay components to minimize experimental variability. The higher sensitivity of the NIF is mainly due to the higher Ab immobilization density via APTES-assisted physical adsorption, where APTES serves as a diluting and binding agent for anti-HFA capture Ab apart from a surface functionalization agent. The detection of HFA spiked in diluted human whole blood and plasma (Figure 2c) using the NIF exhibited similar precision as the commercial kit, showing its applicability for the determination of HFA in clinical and bioanalytical settings. The NIF is the most sensitive immunoassay format in comparison to the CIF and CovIF for HFA. The stability of Ab immobilized on MTPs was excellent after two months of storage at 4 ºC in 0.1 M PBS (pH 7.4). No significant decrease in the Ab functional activity was observed after eight weeks (Figure 2d), evincing the leach-proof Ab immobilization. The preparation is very rapid (1 h for Ab immobilization and blocking), therefore, it might obviate the preparation of Ab-bound MTPs in priori considering most antibodies are only good within two weeks of storage.
The authors declare no competing financial interests.

One-step antibody immobilization-based rapid and highly-sensitive sandwich ELISA procedure for potential in vitro diagnostics by Sandeep Kumar Vashist, E. Marion Schneider, Edmond Lam, Sabahudin Hrapovic, and John H. T. Luong published in Scientific Reports on 18 Mar, 2014 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/srep04407

Figure 1. One-step antibody immobilization-based sandwich ELISA procedure for the detection of human fetuin A (HFA)^11^. Figure 2. Bioanalytical performance of the developed sandwich ELISA procedure (a) Detection of HFA by the new immobilization format (NIF)^11^, conventional immobilization format (CIF) and covalent immobilization format (CovIF)^6^ based sandwich ELISA procedures. (b) Specific HFA detection with respect to various experimental process controls. (c) Detection of HFA spiked in various sample matrices: PBS (0.1 M, pH 7.4), diluted human whole blood and diluted human plasma. (d) Assessment of stability of anti-HFA Ab-bound MTPs stored in 0.1 M PBS, pH 7.4 at 4°C for 8 weeks in terms of HFA detection. All experiments were done in triplicate, while the error bars represent the standard deviation. Figure 3. Table 1 Troubleshooting Table Figure 4. Table 2 Analytical comparison of the new immobilization format (NIF)^11^ with the conventional immobilization format (CIF) and covalent immobilization format (CovIF)^6^ based sandwich ELISAs for the detection of HFA
